   180‚Üí    # Disconnect all sandboxes first
   181‚Üí    user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true).find_each do |sandbox|
   182‚Üí      disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   183‚Üí    end
   184‚Üí
   185‚Üí    cleanup_sidecar(user)
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí    user.update!(
   188‚Üí      tailscale_state: "disabled",
   189‚Üí      tailscale_container_id: nil,
   190‚Üí      tailscale_network: nil
   191‚Üí    )
   192‚Üí    delete_auth_key(user)
   193‚Üí    user
   194‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   195‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to disable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   196‚Üí  end
   197‚Üí
   198‚Üí  def status(user:)
   199‚Üí    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" unless user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
   200‚Üí    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
   203‚Üí    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí    ts_sandboxes = user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true)
   206‚Üí    sandbox_ips = ts_sandboxes.map do |sb|
   207‚Üí      ip = sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sb)
   208‚Üí      { name: sb.name, ip: ip }
   209‚Üí    end
   210‚Üí
   211‚Üí    result = {
   212‚Üí      running: running,
   213‚Üí      container_id: user.tailscale_container_id[0..11],
   214‚Üí      network: user.tailscale_network,
   215‚Üí      connected_sandboxes: ts_sandboxes.count,
   216‚Üí      sandboxes: sandbox_ips
   217‚Üí    }
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí    if running
   220‚Üí      ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
   221‚Üí      result[:tailscale_ip] = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?
   222‚Üí
   223‚Üí      status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
   224‚Üí      if status_out.first.any?
   225‚Üí        begin
   226‚Üí          ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
   227‚Üí          result[:hostname] = ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName")
   228‚Üí          result[:tailnet] = ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
   229‚Üí          result[:online] = ts_status.dig("Self", "Online")
   230‚Üí          tags = ts_status.dig("Self", "Tags")
   231‚Üí          result[:tags] = tags if tags.present?
   232‚Üí        rescue JSON::ParserError
   233‚Üí          # Status not available yet
   234‚Üí        end
   235‚Üí      end
   236‚Üí    else
   237‚Üí      # Grab last 20 lines of container logs for debugging
   238‚Üí      logs = container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, tail: 20)
   239‚Üí      result[:logs] = logs.encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace).strip if logs.present?
   240‚Üí      result[:exit_code] = container.json.dig("State", "ExitCode")
   241‚Üí      result[:error_reason] = container.json.dig("State", "Error")
   242‚Üí    end
   243‚Üí
   244‚Üí    result
   245‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   246‚Üí    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found ‚ÄĒ try disabling and re-enabling Tailscale"
   247‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   248‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to get Tailscale status: #{e.message}"
   249‚Üí  end
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí  def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
   252‚Üí    user = sandbox.user
   253‚Üí    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
   254‚Üí    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?
   255‚Üí
   256‚Üí    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
   257‚Üí    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
   258‚Üí    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
   259‚Üí    sandbox
   260‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   261‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   262‚Üí  end
   263‚Üí
   264‚Üí  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
   265‚Üí    user = sandbox.user
   266‚Üí    return sandbox unless sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_network.present?
   267‚Üí
   268‚Üí    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   269‚Üí      begin
   270‚Üí        network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
   271‚Üí        network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
   272‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
   273‚Üí        # Network/container already gone or container not connected ‚ÄĒ desired state reached
   274‚Üí      end
   275‚Üí    end
   276‚Üí
   277‚Üí    sandbox.update!(tailscale: false)
   278‚Üí    sandbox
   279‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   280‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to disconnect sandbox from Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   281‚Üí  end
   282‚Üí
   283‚Üí  def sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox:)
   284‚Üí    user = sandbox.user
   285‚Üí    return nil unless sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.container_id.present? && user.tailscale_network.present?
   286‚Üí
   287‚Üí    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   288‚Üí    container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", user.tailscale_network, "IPAddress")
   289‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   290‚Üí    nil
   291‚Üí  end
   292‚Üí
   293‚Üí  def auth_key_path(user)
   294‚Üí    File.join(DATA_DIR, "users", user.name, "tailscale", ".auth_key")
   295‚Üí  end
   296‚Üí
   297‚Üí  private
   298‚Üí
   299‚Üí  def save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
   300‚Üí    path = auth_key_path(user)
   301‚Üí    FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(path))
   302‚Üí    File.write(path, auth_key)
   303‚Üí    File.chmod(0o600, path)
   304‚Üí  end
   305‚Üí
   306‚Üí  def delete_auth_key(user)
   307‚Üí    path = auth_key_path(user)
   308‚Üí    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
   309‚Üí  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   310‚Üí    # already gone
   311‚Üí  end
   312‚Üí
   313‚Üí  def create_and_start_sidecar(user:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
   314‚Üí    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   315‚Üí    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   316‚Üí    subnet = subnet_for(user)
   317‚Üí
   318‚Üí    pull_image
   319‚Üí    create_network(network_name, subnet)
   320‚Üí    remove_existing_container(container_name)
   321‚Üí    clear_tailscale_state(user)
   322‚Üí    container = create_sidecar(
   323‚Üí      name: container_name,
   324‚Üí      user: user,
   325‚Üí      network: network_name,
   326‚Üí      subnet: subnet,
   327‚Üí      auth_key: auth_key,
   328‚Üí      hostname: hostname
   329‚Üí    )
   330‚Üí    container.start
   331‚Üí
   332‚Üí    # Persist the subnet so it survives reinstalls (subnet_for reads this first).
   333‚Üí    user.update_column(:tailscale_subnet, subnet)
   334‚Üí
   335‚Üí    [ network_name, container ]
   336‚Üí  end
   337‚Üí
   338‚Üí  def remove_existing_container(name)
   339‚Üí    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
   340‚Üí    container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   341‚Üí    container.delete(force: true)
   342‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   343‚Üí    # No existing container
   344‚Üí  end
   345‚Üí
   346‚Üí  # Remove stale tailscaled.state so the daemon starts fresh.
   347‚Üí  # Stale state causes tailscaled to immediately set up VPN routes before
   348‚Üí  # authentication, which silently drops all normal internet traffic.
   349‚Üí  def clear_tailscale_state(user)
   350‚Üí    state_file = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale/tailscaled.state"
   351‚Üí    File.delete(state_file) if File.exist?(state_file)
   352‚Üí  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   353‚Üí    # already gone
   354‚Üí  end
   355‚Üí
   356‚Üí  def cleanup_sidecar(user)
   357‚Üí    # Try by stored container ID first, then by name as fallback
   358‚Üí    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   359‚Üí    [user.tailscale_container_id, container_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
   360‚Üí      begin
   361‚Üí        container = Docker::Container.get(ref)
   362‚Üí        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   363‚Üí        container.delete(force: true)
   364‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   365‚Üí        # Already gone
   366‚Üí      end
   367‚Üí    end
   368‚Üí
   369‚Üí    # Clean up network by stored name, then by convention
   370‚Üí    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   371‚Üí    [user.tailscale_network, network_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
   372‚Üí      begin
   373‚Üí        Docker::Network.get(ref).delete
   374‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   375‚Üí        # Already gone
   376‚Üí      end
   377‚Üí    end
   378‚Üí  end
   379‚Üí
   380‚Üí  def cleanup_on_failure(user)
   381‚Üí    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   382‚Üí    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   383‚Üí    begin
   384‚Üí      Docker::Container.get(container_name).delete(force: true)
   385‚Üí    rescue StandardError
   386‚Üí      nil
   387‚Üí    end
   388‚Üí    begin
   389‚Üí      Docker::Network.get(network_name).delete
   390‚Üí    rescue StandardError
   391‚Üí      nil
   392‚Üí    end
   393‚Üí    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
   394‚Üí  end
   395‚Üí
   396‚Üí  def pull_image
   397‚Üí    Docker::Image.get(TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
   398‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   399‚Üí    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
   400‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   401‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{TAILSCALE_IMAGE} ‚ÄĒ check network connectivity"
   402‚Üí  end
   403‚Üí
   404‚Üí  def subnet_for(user)
   405‚Üí    # 1. Use the subnet stored in the DB ‚ÄĒ stable across Docker/reinstalls
   406‚Üí    return user.tailscale_subnet if user.tailscale_subnet.present?
   407‚Üí
   408‚Üí    # 2. If the network already exists on Docker, read its actual subnet
   409‚Üí    begin
   410‚Üí      network = Docker::Network.get("sc-ts-net-#{user.name}")
   411‚Üí      ipam = network.info.dig("IPAM", "Config")
   412‚Üí      return ipam.first["Subnet"] if ipam&.first
   413‚Üí    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   414‚Üí      # Network doesn't exist yet ‚ÄĒ fall through to generate a random /24
   415‚Üí    end
   416‚Üí
   417‚Üí    # 3. Generate a random /24 from the pool (first allocation)
   418‚Üí    base = ENV["DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"]
   419‚Üí    if base
   420‚Üí      parts = base.split("/").first.split(".").map(&:to_i)
   421‚Üí    else
   422‚Üí      parts = [ 10, rand(1..254), 0, 0 ]
   423‚Üí    end
   424‚Üí    parts[2] = rand(1..254)
   425‚Üí    "#{parts[0]}.#{parts[1]}.#{parts[2]}.0/24"
   426‚Üí  end
   427‚Üí
   428‚Üí  def create_network(name, subnet)
   429‚Üí    Docker::Network.get(name)
   430‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   431‚Üí    Docker::Network.create(
   432‚Üí      name,
   433‚Üí      "Driver" => "bridge",
   434‚Üí      "IPAM" => {
   435‚Üí        "Config" => [ { "Subnet" => subnet } ]
   436‚Üí      }
   437‚Üí    )
   438‚Üí  end
   439‚Üí
   440‚Üí  def create_sidecar(name:, user:, network:, subnet:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
   441‚Üí    state_dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale"
   442‚Üí    ts_hostname = hostname.presence || TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME
   443‚Üí
   444‚Üí    config = {
   445‚Üí      "Image" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE,
   446‚Üí      "name" => name,
   447‚Üí      "Hostname" => ts_hostname,
   448‚Üí      "HostConfig" => {
   449‚Üí        "NetworkMode" => network,
   450‚Üí        "CapAdd" => [ "NET_ADMIN", "SYS_MODULE" ],
   451‚Üí        "Devices" => [
   452‚Üí          { "PathOnHost" => "/dev/net/tun", "PathInContainer" => "/dev/net/tun", "CgroupPermissions" => "rwm" }
   453‚Üí        ],
   454‚Üí        "Sysctls" => { "net.ipv4.ip_forward" => "1" },
   455‚Üí        "Binds" => [ "#{state_dir}:/var/lib/tailscale" ],
   456‚Üí        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   457‚Üí      }
   458‚Üí    }
   459‚Üí
   460‚Üí    if auth_key.present?
   461‚Üí      # Use containerboot (default entrypoint) with auth key for automated flow
   462‚Üí      config["Env"] = [
   463‚Üí        "TS_STATE_DIR=/var/lib/tailscale",
   464‚Üí        "TS_HOSTNAME=#{ts_hostname}",
   465‚Üí        "TS_EXTRA_ARGS=--advertise-routes=#{subnet} --accept-routes#{TAILSCALE_TAG ? " --advertise-tags=#{TAILSCALE_TAG}" : ""}",
   466‚Üí        "TS_AUTH_ONCE=true",
   467‚Üí        "TS_AUTHKEY=#{auth_key}"
   468‚Üí      ]
   469‚Üí    else
   470‚Üí      # Run tailscaled directly ‚ÄĒ we manage login via `tailscale up`.
   471‚Üí      # Also used for restore_from_state: raw tailscaled exits without running
   472‚Üí      # `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, preserving saved credentials in state file.
   473‚Üí      config["Entrypoint"] = [ "tailscaled", "--state=/var/lib/tailscale/tailscaled.state" ]
   474‚Üí      config["Cmd"] = []
   475‚Üí    end
   476‚Üí
   477‚Üí    Docker::Container.create(config)
   478‚Üí  end
   479‚Üíend
   480‚Üí

1‚Üíclass SandboxProvisionJob < ApplicationJob
     2‚Üí  queue_as :default
     3‚Üí
     4‚Üí  def perform(sandbox_id:)
     5‚Üí    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)
     6‚Üí    return if sandbox.status == "running" # Idempotent
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí    sandbox.start_job("creating")
     9‚Üí    manager = SandboxManager.new
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí    begin
    12‚Üí      # Directory creation
    13‚Üí      manager.ensure_mount_dirs(sandbox.user, sandbox)
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí      # Image pull (can be slow)
    16‚Üí      manager.ensure_image(sandbox.image)
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí      # Container creation and start
    19‚Üí      manager.create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: sandbox.user)
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí      # Connect to Tailscale if enabled
    22‚Üí      if sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.user.tailscale_enabled?
    23‚Üí        TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    24‚Üí      end
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí      sandbox.update!(status: "running")
    27‚Üí      sandbox.finish_job
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí    rescue => e
    30‚Üí      Rails.logger.error("SandboxProvisionJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
    31‚Üí      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to create: #{e.message}")
    32‚Üí      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed")
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí      raise # Re-raise for Solid Queue retry
    35‚Üí    end
    36‚Üí  end
    37‚Üíend
    38‚Üí

1‚Üíclass SandboxManager
     2‚Üí  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3‚Üí  DEFAULT_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest"
     4‚Üí  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
     5‚Üí
     6‚Üí  class Error < StandardError; end
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí  def create(user:, name:, image: DEFAULT_IMAGE, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)
     9‚Üí    # Build sandbox record (not saved yet)
    10‚Üí    sandbox = user.sandboxes.build(
    11‚Üí      name: name,
    12‚Üí      image: image,
    13‚Üí      status: "pending",
    14‚Üí      mount_home: mount_home,
    15‚Üí      data_path: data_path,
    16‚Üí      temporary: temporary
    17‚Üí    )
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí    # Validate before doing expensive operations
    20‚Üí    sandbox.validate!
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí    # Create directories FIRST (can fail fast before saving record)
    23‚Üí    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí    # Now safe to save
    26‚Üí    sandbox.save!
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí    # Pull image
    29‚Üí    ensure_image(image)
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí    # Record image digest and build date
    32‚Üí    info = fetch_image_info(image)
    33‚Üí    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí    # Create and start container
    36‚Üí    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí    # Connect to Tailscale if requested
    39‚Üí    if (tailscale || user.tailscale_auto_connect?) && user.tailscale_enabled?
    40‚Üí      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    41‚Üí    end
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí    sandbox
    44‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    45‚Üí    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    46‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
    47‚Üí  rescue => e
    48‚Üí    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    49‚Üí    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed
    50‚Üí    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    51‚Üí    raise Error, e.message
    52‚Üí  end
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí  # Public method for job usage
    55‚Üí  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    56‚Üí    container = Docker::Container.create(
    57‚Üí      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
    58‚Üí      "Image" => sandbox.image,
    59‚Üí      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
    60‚Üí      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
    61‚Üí      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
    62‚Üí      "HostConfig" => {
    63‚Üí        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
    64‚Üí        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
    65‚Üí        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
    66‚Üí        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
    67‚Üí      },
    68‚Üí      "NetworkingConfig" => {
    69‚Üí        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
    70‚Üí      }
    71‚Üí    )
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí    container.start
    74‚Üí    container.refresh!
    75‚Üí    unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    76‚Üí      state_error = container.json.dig("State", "Error").presence || container.json.dig("State", "Status")
    77‚Üí      container.stop rescue nil
    78‚Üí      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
    79‚Üí      raise Error, "Container failed to start: #{state_error}"
    80‚Üí    end
    81‚Üí    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí    # Connect to per-user network for tenant isolation
    84‚Üí    begin
    85‚Üí      NetworkManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    86‚Üí    rescue NetworkManager::Error => e
    87‚Üí      container.stop rescue nil
    88‚Üí      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
    89‚Üí      sandbox.update!(container_id: nil, status: "pending")
    90‚Üí      raise Error, "Failed to attach tenant network: #{e.message}"
    91‚Üí    end
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    94‚Üí    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    95‚Üí    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí    # Set SMB password via exec (avoids leaking password in container env/metadata)
    98‚Üí    set_smb_password(container, sandbox.user) if sandbox.smb_enabled?
    99‚Üí  end
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí  # Public method for job usage
   102‚Üí  def ensure_image(image)
   103‚Üí    Docker::Image.get(image)
   104‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   105‚Üí    raise Error, "Snapshot image #{image} not found locally (snapshots are never pulled from a registry)" if image.start_with?("sc-snap-")
   106‚Üí    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
   107‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   108‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
   109‚Üí  end
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí  # Public method for job usage
   112‚Üí  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   113‚Üí    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
   114‚Üí    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí    # Ensure user base directory is writable (may be root-owned from previous install)
   117‚Üí    ensure_dir("#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}")
   118‚Üí
   119‚Üí    if sandbox.mount_home
   120‚Üí      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   121‚Üí      ensure_dir(dir)
   122‚Üí      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   123‚Üí    end
   124‚Üí    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   125‚Üí      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
   126‚Üí      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üí      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   129‚Üí      ensure_dir(dir)
   130‚Üí      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   131‚Üí    end
   132‚Üí    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
   133‚Üí    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   134‚Üí      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   135‚Üí      ensure_dir(dir)
   136‚Üí      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   137‚Üí    end
   138‚Üí  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
   139‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
   140‚Üí  end
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí  def destroy(sandbox:, archive: false)
   143‚Üí    begin
   144‚Üí      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   145‚Üí    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   146‚Üí      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   147‚Üí    end
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí    begin
   150‚Üí      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   151‚Üí    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   152‚Üí      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   153‚Üí    end
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üí    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí    if sandbox.tailscale?
   158‚Üí      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   159‚Üí    end
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   162‚Üí      begin
   163‚Üí        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   164‚Üí        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   165‚Üí        container.delete(force: true)
   166‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   167‚Üí        # Container already gone
   168‚Üí      end
   169‚Üí    end
   170‚Üí
   171‚Üí    if archive
   172‚Üí      # Soft-delete: keep volume on disk, mark as archived.
   173‚Üí      # Rename to free the original name for reuse.
   174‚Üí      archived_name = "#{Time.current.strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}-#{sandbox.name}"
   175‚Üí      sandbox.update!(status: "archived", container_id: nil, archived_at: Time.current, name: archived_name)
   176‚Üí    else
   177‚Üí      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   178‚Üí    end
   179‚Üí
   180‚Üí    # Remove per-user network if this was the last active sandbox
   181‚Üí    begin
   182‚Üí      NetworkManager.new.cleanup_user_network(sandbox.user)
   183‚Üí    rescue NetworkManager::Error => e
   184‚Üí      Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager#destroy: network cleanup failed for #{sandbox.user.name}: #{e.message}")
   185‚Üí    end
   186‚Üí  end
   187‚Üí
   188‚Üí  # Restore an archived sandbox: recreate the container from the existing volume.
   189‚Üí  # The container is started immediately; status is set to "running".
   190‚Üí  def restore_from_archive(sandbox:)
   191‚Üí    raise Error, "Sandbox is not archived" unless sandbox.status == "archived"
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí    user = sandbox.user
   194‚Üí
   195‚Üí    ensure_image(sandbox.image)
   196‚Üí    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   197‚Üí
   198‚Üí    info = fetch_image_info(sandbox.image)
   199‚Üí    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])
   200‚Üí
   201‚Üí    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   202‚Üí    # Strip the timestamp prefix added during archival (e.g. "20260307123456-mybox" ‚Üí "mybox")
   203‚Üí    original_name = sandbox.name.sub(/\A\d{14}-/, "")
   204‚Üí    sandbox.update!(archived_at: nil, name: original_name)
   205‚Üí
   206‚Üí    if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   207‚Üí      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   208‚Üí    end
   209‚Üí
   210‚Üí    sandbox
   211‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   212‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to restore archived sandbox: #{e.message}"
   213‚Üí  end
   214‚Üí
   215‚Üí  def start(sandbox:)
   216‚Üí    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
   217‚Üí    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí    user = sandbox.user
   220‚Üí
   221‚Üí    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   222‚Üí      begin
   223‚Üí        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   224‚Üí        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   225‚Üí        old.delete(force: true)
   226‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   227‚Üí        # already gone
   228‚Üí      end
   229‚Üí    end
   230‚Üí
   231‚Üí    # Reset bind-mount directory permissions before starting the new container.
   232‚Üí    # After a previous Sysbox run, dirs may be owned by a remapped UID that
   233‚Üí    # Rails can't chmod ‚ÄĒ ensure_mount_dirs uses sudo to reset to 777.
   234‚Üí    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   235‚Üí
   236‚Üí    # Refresh image info in case the image was updated since sandbox creation
   237‚Üí    info = fetch_image_info(sandbox.image)
   238‚Üí    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])
   239‚Üí
   240‚Üí    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   241‚Üí
   242‚Üí    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   243‚Üí    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí    sandbox
   246‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   247‚Üí    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   248‚Üí    raise Error, "Container not found ‚ÄĒ sandbox must be recreated"
   249‚Üí  end
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí  def stop(sandbox:)
   252‚Üí    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"
   253‚Üí
   254‚Üí    begin
   255‚Üí      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   256‚Üí    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   257‚Üí      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   258‚Üí    end
   259‚Üí
   260‚Üí    begin
   261‚Üí      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   262‚Üí    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   263‚Üí      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   264‚Üí    end
   265‚Üí
   266‚Üí    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   267‚Üí
   268‚Üí    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   269‚Üí      begin
   270‚Üí        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   271‚Üí        container.stop(t: 10)
   272‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   273‚Üí        # already gone
   274‚Üí      end
   275‚Üí    end
   276‚Üí
   277‚Üí    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
   278‚Üí    sandbox
   279‚Üí  end
   280‚Üí
   281‚Üí  SERVICES = %w[docker vnc].freeze
   282‚Üí
   283‚Üí  def service_start(sandbox:, service:)
   284‚Üí    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
   285‚Üí    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   286‚Üí
   287‚Üí    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   288‚Üí    user = sandbox.user.name
   289‚Üí
   290‚Üí    case service
   291‚Üí    when "docker"
   292‚Üí      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x dockerd > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; dockerd --storage-driver=overlay2 --mtu=$(ip link show eth0 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'mtu \\K[0-9]+' || echo 1500) &>/var/log/dockerd.log & echo started" ])
   293‚Üí    when "vnc"
   294‚Üí      geometry = sandbox.vnc_geometry || "1280x900"
   295‚Üí      depth = sandbox.vnc_depth || 24
   296‚Üí      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x Xvnc > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'Xvnc :99 -rfbport 5900 -SecurityTypes None -AlwaysShared -geometry #{geometry} -depth #{depth} &>/var/log/xvnc.log &' && su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'DISPLAY=:99 openbox &>/var/log/openbox.log &' && websockify -addr :6080 -target localhost:5900 -url /websockify &>/var/log/websockify.log & echo started" ])
   297‚Üí    end
   298‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   299‚Üí    raise Error, "Container not found"
   300‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   301‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to start #{service}: #{e.message}"
   302‚Üí  end
   303‚Üí
   304‚Üí  def service_stop(sandbox:, service:)
   305‚Üí    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
   306‚Üí    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   307‚Üí
   308‚Üí    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   309‚Üí
   310‚Üí    case service
   311‚Üí    when "docker"
   312‚Üí      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x dockerd 2>/dev/null; pkill -x containerd 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
   313‚Üí    when "vnc"
   314‚Üí      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x websockify 2>/dev/null; pkill -x openbox 2>/dev/null; pkill -x Xvnc 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
   315‚Üí    end
   316‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   317‚Üí    raise Error, "Container not found"
   318‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   319‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to stop #{service}: #{e.message}"
   320‚Üí  end
   321‚Üí
   322‚Üí  def logs(sandbox:, tail: 200, timestamps: false)
   323‚Üí    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   324‚Üí
   325‚Üí    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   326‚Üí    container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, follow: false, tail: tail, timestamps: timestamps)
   327‚Üí      .force_encoding("UTF-8").encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace, replace: "")
   328‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   329‚Üí    raise Error, "Container not found"
   330‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   331‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to fetch logs: #{e.message}"
   332‚Üí  end
   333‚Üí
   334‚Üí  def status(sandbox:)
   335‚Üí    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   336‚Üí
   337‚Üí    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   338‚Üí    info = container.json
   339‚Üí    {
   340‚Üí      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
   341‚Üí      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
   342‚Üí      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
   343‚Üí      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
   344‚Üí    }
   345‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   346‚Üí    { state: "not_found" }
   347‚Üí  end
   348‚Üí
   349‚Üí  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
   350‚Üí  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
   351‚Üí  #
   352‚Üí  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data"
   353‚Üí  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
   354‚Üí  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
   355‚Üí    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   356‚Üí
   357‚Üí    user = sandbox.user
   358‚Üí    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
   359‚Üí    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data]
   360‚Üí
   361‚Üí    snap = Snapshot.new(
   362‚Üí      user: user,
   363‚Üí      name: name,
   364‚Üí      label: label,
   365‚Üí      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
   366‚Üí      data_subdir: data_subdir
   367‚Üí    )
   368‚Üí
   369‚Üí    # ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Container layer ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
   370‚Üí    if requested_layers.include?("container")
   371‚Üí      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   372‚Üí      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   373‚Üí      image = container.commit(
   374‚Üí        repo: repo,
   375‚Üí        tag: name,
   376‚Üí        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
   377‚Üí      )
   378‚Üí      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
   379‚Üí      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
   380‚Üí    end
   381‚Üí
   382‚Üí    # ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Home layer (BTRFS only) ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
   383‚Üí    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   384‚Üí      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   385‚Üí      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
   386‚Üí      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
   387‚Üí        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
   388‚Üí        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
   389‚Üí        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
   390‚Üí      end
   391‚Üí    end
   392‚Üí
   393‚Üí    # ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Data layer (BTRFS only) ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
   394‚Üí    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   395‚Üí      if data_subdir.present?
   396‚Üí        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   397‚Üí      else
   398‚Üí        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   399‚Üí      end
   400‚Üí      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
   401‚Üí      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
   402‚Üí        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
   403‚Üí        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
   404‚Üí        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
   405‚Üí      end
   406‚Üí    end
   407‚Üí
   408‚Üí    snap.save!
   409‚Üí    snap
   410‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   411‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
   412‚Üí  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   413‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
   414‚Üí  end
   415‚Üí
   416‚Üí  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
   417‚Üí  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
   418‚Üí    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
   419‚Üí    {
   420‚Üí      name: snap.name,
   421‚Üí      image: snap.docker_image,
   422‚Üí      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   423‚Üí      created_at: snap.created_at
   424‚Üí    }
   425‚Üí  end
   426‚Üí
   427‚Üí  def list_snapshots(user:)
   428‚Üí    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   429‚Üí    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
   430‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   431‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
   432‚Üí  end
   433‚Üí
   434‚Üí  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
   435‚Üí    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   436‚Üí    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
   437‚Üí  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   438‚Üí    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   439‚Üí  end
   440‚Üí
   441‚Üí  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
   442‚Üí    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)
   443‚Üí
   444‚Üí    if snap
   445‚Üí      # Remove Docker image
   446‚Üí      if snap.docker_image.present?
   447‚Üí        begin
   448‚Üí          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
   449‚Üí        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   450‚Üí          # Already gone
   451‚Üí        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   452‚Üí          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
   453‚Üí        end
   454‚Üí      end
   455‚Üí
   456‚Üí      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
   457‚Üí      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
   458‚Üí        begin
   459‚Üí          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
   460‚Üí        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   461‚Üí          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   462‚Üí        end
   463‚Üí      end
   464‚Üí
   465‚Üí      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
   466‚Üí        begin
   467‚Üí          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
   468‚Üí        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   469‚Üí          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   470‚Üí        end
   471‚Üí      end
   472‚Üí
   473‚Üí      snap.destroy!
   474‚Üí    else
   475‚Üí      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
   476‚Üí      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
   477‚Üí      begin
   478‚Üí        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
   479‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   480‚Üí        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   481‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   482‚Üí        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
   483‚Üí      end
   484‚Üí    end
   485‚Üí  end
   486‚Üí
   487‚Üí  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
   488‚Üí    user = sandbox.user
   489‚Üí    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?
   490‚Üí
   491‚Üí    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
   492‚Üí    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)
   493‚Üí
   494‚Üí    # Determine the Docker image to use
   495‚Üí    if snap&.docker_image.present?
   496‚Üí      image_ref = snap.docker_image
   497‚Üí    else
   498‚Üí      # Fall back to legacy naming
   499‚Üí      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
   500‚Üí    end
   501‚Üí
   502‚Üí    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
   503‚Üí    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
   504‚Üí    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))
   505‚Üí
   506‚Üí    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
   507‚Üí    if restore_container
   508‚Üí      begin
   509‚Üí        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   510‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   511‚Üí        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
   512‚Üí      end
   513‚Üí    end
   514‚Üí
   515‚Üí    begin
   516‚Üí      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   517‚Üí    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   518‚Üí      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   519‚Üí    end
   520‚Üí
   521‚Üí    begin
   522‚Üí      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   523‚Üí    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   524‚Üí      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   525‚Üí    end
   526‚Üí
   527‚Üí    if sandbox.tailscale?
   528‚Üí      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   529‚Üí    end
   530‚Üí
   531‚Üí    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   532‚Üí      begin
   533‚Üí        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   534‚Üí        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   535‚Üí        old_container.delete(force: true)
   536‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   537‚Üí        # Already gone
   538‚Üí      end
   539‚Üí    end
   540‚Üí
   541‚Üí    # ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Restore home directory ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
   542‚Üí    if restore_home
   543‚Üí      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   544‚Üí      begin
   545‚Üí        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
   546‚Üí      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   547‚Üí        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
   548‚Üí      end
   549‚Üí    end
   550‚Üí
   551‚Üí    # ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Restore data directory ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
   552‚Üí    if restore_data
   553‚Üí      if snap.data_subdir.present?
   554‚Üí        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   555‚Üí      else
   556‚Üí        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   557‚Üí      end
   558‚Üí      begin
   559‚Üí        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
   560‚Üí      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   561‚Üí        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
   562‚Üí      end
   563‚Üí    end
   564‚Üí
   565‚Üí    # ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Recreate container ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
   566‚Üí    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
   567‚Üí    # Clear stale container reference before recreating ‚ÄĒ prevents the record
   568‚Üí    # from pointing at a dead container if create_container_and_start fails.
   569‚Üí    sandbox.update!(image: final_image, container_id: nil, status: "pending")
   570‚Üí
   571‚Üí    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   572‚Üí
   573‚Üí    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
   574‚Üí      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   575‚Üí    end
   576‚Üí
   577‚Üí    sandbox
   578‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   579‚Üí    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
   580‚Üí  end
   581‚Üí
   582‚Üí  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
   583‚Üí  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   584‚Üí    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   585‚Üí
   586‚Üí    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
   587‚Üí      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
   588‚Üí      repo_tags.each do |tag|
   589‚Üí        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
   590‚Üí        next unless repo == repo_prefix
   591‚Üí        next if tag_name.blank?
   592‚Üí        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)
   593‚Üí
   594‚Üí        Snapshot.create!(
   595‚Üí          user: user,
   596‚Üí          name: tag_name,
   597‚Üí          docker_image: tag,
   598‚Üí          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
   599‚Üí          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
   600‚Üí          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
   601‚Üí        )
   602‚Üí      end
   603‚Üí    end
   604‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
   605‚Üí    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
   606‚Üí  end
   607‚Üí
   608‚Üí  def snapshot_json(snap)
   609‚Üí    {
   610‚Üí      name: snap.name,
   611‚Üí      label: snap.label,
   612‚Üí      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   613‚Üí      layers: snap.layers,
   614‚Üí      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
   615‚Üí      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
   616‚Üí      home_size: snap.home_size,
   617‚Üí      data_size: snap.data_size,
   618‚Üí      total_size: snap.total_size,
   619‚Üí      created_at: snap.created_at
   620‚Üí    }
   621‚Üí  end
   622‚Üí
   623‚Üí  def connect_info(sandbox:)
   624‚Üí    user = sandbox.user.name
   625‚Üí
   626‚Üí    unless sandbox.tailscale?
   627‚Üí      raise Error, "SSH access requires Tailscale. Enable Tailscale on your account and connect this sandbox to your tailnet."
   628‚Üí    end
   629‚Üí
   630‚Üí    ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   631‚Üí    raise Error, "Tailscale IP not available ‚ÄĒ is the Tailscale sidecar running?" unless ts_ip.present?
   632‚Üí
   633‚Üí    {
   634‚Üí      host: ts_ip,
   635‚Üí      port: 22,
   636‚Üí      user: user,
   637‚Üí      command: "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}",
   638‚Üí      tailscale_ip: ts_ip
   639‚Üí    }
   640‚Üí  end
   641‚Üí
   642‚Üí  # Update the SMB password on all active SMB-enabled sandboxes for a user.
   643‚Üí  def update_smb_password(user:)
   644‚Üí    raise Error, "No SMB password set" unless user.smb_password.present?
   645‚Üí
   646‚Üí    user.sandboxes.active.where(smb_enabled: true).find_each do |sandbox|
   647‚Üí      next if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   648‚Üí
   649‚Üí      begin
   650‚Üí        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   651‚Üí        set_smb_password(container, user)
   652‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   653‚Üí        # Container gone, skip
   654‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   655‚Üí        Rails.logger.warn("Failed to update SMB password for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
   656‚Üí      end
   657‚Üí    end
   658‚Üí  end
   659‚Üí
   660‚Üí  private
   661‚Üí
   662‚Üí  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
   663‚Üí    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
   664‚Üí    max_attempts.times do
   665‚Üí      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status
   666‚Üí
   667‚Üí      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
   668‚Üí      if sandbox.status != "running"
   669‚Üí        sleep delay
   670‚Üí        next
   671‚Üí      end
   672‚Üí
   673‚Üí      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
   674‚Üí      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   675‚Üí      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?
   676‚Üí
   677‚Üí      sleep delay
   678‚Üí    end
   679‚Üí    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
   680‚Üí    nil
   681‚Üí  end
   682‚Üí
   683‚Üí  # Extracts the image SHA and BUILD_DATE from a local Docker image.
   684‚Üí  # Returns a hash with :image_id and :image_built_at (or nils on error).
   685‚Üí  def fetch_image_info(image_ref)
   686‚Üí    img    = Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   687‚Üí    config = img.json.dig("Config") || {}
   688‚Üí    labels = config["Labels"] || {}
   689‚Üí
   690‚Üí    date_str = labels["org.opencontainers.image.created"] || labels["BUILD_DATE"]
   691‚Üí    if date_str.nil?
   692‚Üí      env_entry = (config["Env"] || []).find { |e| e.start_with?("BUILD_DATE=") }
   693‚Üí      date_str  = env_entry&.split("=", 2)&.last
   694‚Üí    end
   695‚Üí
   696‚Üí    {
   697‚Üí      image_id:       img.id,
   698‚Üí      image_built_at: date_str ? Time.zone.parse(date_str) : nil,
   699‚Üí      image_version:  labels["org.opencontainers.image.version"]
   700‚Üí    }
   701‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ArgumentError, TypeError
   702‚Üí    { image_id: nil, image_built_at: nil, image_version: nil }
   703‚Üí  end
   704‚Üí
   705‚Üí  def connect_to_network(container)
   706‚Üí    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   707‚Üí    network.connect(container.id)
   708‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   709‚Üí    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: network #{NETWORK_NAME} not found, skipping network connection")
   710‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   711‚Üí    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: failed to connect container to #{NETWORK_NAME}: #{e.message}")
   712‚Üí  end

   714‚Üí  def container_env(user, sandbox)
   715‚Üí    env = [
   716‚Üí      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
   717‚Üí      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
   718‚Üí    ]
   719‚Üí    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
   720‚Üí    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
   721‚Üí    env << "GITHUB_USERNAME=#{user.github_username}" if user.github_username.present?
   722‚Üí    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   723‚Üí    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
   724‚Üí    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
   725‚Üí    env << "SANDCASTLE_DOCKER_ENABLED=#{sandbox.docker_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   726‚Üí    env << "SANDCASTLE_SMB_ENABLED=#{sandbox.smb_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   727‚Üí    env << "SANDCASTLE_HOME_PERSISTED=#{sandbox.mount_home ? '1' : '0'}"
   728‚Üí    env << "SANDCASTLE_DATA_PERSISTED=#{sandbox.data_path.present? ? '1' : '0'}"
   729‚Üí    env << "SANDCASTLE_DATA_PATH=#{sandbox.data_path}" if sandbox.data_path.present?
   730‚Üí    env
   731‚Üí  end
   732‚Üí
   733‚Üí  def container_runtime
   734‚Üí    @container_runtime ||= begin
   735‚Üí      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
   736‚Üí      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
   737‚Üí        "sysbox-runc"
   738‚Üí      else
   739‚Üí        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
   740‚Üí        "runc"
   741‚Üí      end
   742‚Üí    end
   743‚Üí  end
   744‚Üí
   745‚Üí  # mkdir_p with self-healing: if EACCES, fix the parent dir's ownership
   746‚Üí  # via a short-lived Docker container (only way when running as non-root
   747‚Üí  # inside a container with Docker socket access).
   748‚Üí  def ensure_dir(path)
   749‚Üí    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
   750‚Üí  rescue Errno::EACCES
   751‚Üí    docker_chown(File.dirname(path))
   752‚Üí    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
   753‚Üí  end
   754‚Üí
   755‚Üí  # Ensure a bind-mounted directory is world-writable so the sandbox user
   756‚Üí  # (non-root inside the Sysbox container) can write to it.
   757‚Üí  def prepare_bind_mount(path)
   758‚Üí    stat = File.stat(path)
   759‚Üí    return if stat.mode & 0o777 == 0o777
   760‚Üí    return if system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chmod", "777", path)
   761‚Üí    docker_chmod(path, "777")
   762‚Üí  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   763‚Üí    # directory disappeared ‚ÄĒ race condition, ignore
   764‚Üí  end
   765‚Üí
   766‚Üí  # Fix ownership and permissions of a host path via sudo (preferred) or a busybox container fallback.
   767‚Üí  def docker_chown(path)
   768‚Üí    return if system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chown", "#{Process.uid}:#{Process.gid}", path) &&
   769‚Üí             system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chmod", "755", path)
   770‚Üí    docker_run_fix(path, "sh", "-c", "chown #{Process.uid}:#{Process.gid} /mnt && chmod 755 /mnt")
   771‚Üí  end
   772‚Üí
   773‚Üí  def docker_chmod(path, mode)
   774‚Üí    docker_run_fix(path, "chmod", mode, "/mnt")
   775‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   776‚Üí    Rails.logger.warn("docker_chmod(#{path}) failed: #{e.message}")
   777‚Üí  end
   778‚Üí
   779‚Üí  def docker_run_fix(host_path, *cmd)
   780‚Üí    image = fix_image
   781‚Üí    c = Docker::Container.create(
   782‚Üí      "Image" => image, "Cmd" => cmd,
   783‚Üí      "HostConfig" => { "Binds" => [ "#{host_path}:/mnt" ] }
   784‚Üí    )
   785‚Üí    c.start
   786‚Üí    result = c.wait(30)
   787‚Üí    exit_code = result&.dig("StatusCode") || -1
   788‚Üí    unless exit_code == 0
   789‚Üí      raise Error, "docker_run_fix failed (exit #{exit_code}) for #{host_path}: #{cmd.join(' ')}"
   790‚Üí    end
   791‚Üí  ensure
   792‚Üí    c&.delete(force: true) rescue nil
   793‚Üí  end
   794‚Üí
   795‚Üí  # Pick an image guaranteed to be on this Docker daemon.
   796‚Üí  # Prefer busybox (tiny), fall back to alpine, then any local image.
   797‚Üí  def fix_image
   798‚Üí    %w[busybox:latest alpine:latest].each do |img|
   799‚Üí      begin
   800‚Üí        return img if Docker::Image.get(img)
   801‚Üí      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   802‚Üí        next
   803‚Üí      end
   804‚Üí    end
   805‚Üí    # Last resort: use any image already present
   806‚Üí    all = Docker::Image.all
   807‚Üí    raise Error, "No local images available for docker_run_fix" if all.empty?
   808‚Üí    tags = all.first.info["RepoTags"]
   809‚Üí    tags&.first || all.first.id
   810‚Üí  end
   811‚Üí
   812‚Üí  # Inject SMB password into the container via exec, avoiding env var leakage.
   813‚Üí  def set_smb_password(container, user)
   814‚Üí    return unless user.smb_password.present?
   815‚Üí
   816‚Üí    username = user.name
   817‚Üí    password = user.smb_password
   818‚Üí    container.exec(
   819‚Üí      ["bash", "-c", "printf '%s\\n%s\\n' \"$0\" \"$0\" | smbpasswd -a -s \"$1\" 2>&1 || echo 'Warning: smbpasswd failed' >&2",
   820‚Üí       password, username]
   821‚Üí    )
   822‚Üí  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   823‚Üí    Rails.logger.warn("set_smb_password failed for #{user.name}: #{e.message}")
   824‚Üí  end
   825‚Üí
   826‚Üí  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
   827‚Üí    binds = []
   828‚Üí    if sandbox.mount_home
   829‚Üí      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
   830‚Üí    end
   831‚Üí    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   832‚Üí      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   833‚Üí      binds << "#{host_path}:/persisted"
   834‚Üí    end
   835‚Üí    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
   836‚Üí    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   837‚Üí      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   838‚Üí      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
   839‚Üí    end
   840‚Üí    binds
   841‚Üí  end
   842‚Üíend

1‚Üíclass Sandbox < ApplicationRecord
     2‚Üí  include ActionView::RecordIdentifier  # For dom_id in Turbo broadcasts
     3‚Üí
     4‚Üí  belongs_to :user
     5‚Üí  has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy

Perfect! Now I have all the critical information. Let me compile my findings:

## Research Summary: TailscaleManager#connect_sandbox Timing Issue

I've analyzed the full flow and identified the exact timing problem. Here's what I found:

### The Timing Issue

**Location of the error:**
- Error occurs in `TailscaleManager#connect_sandbox` (line 251-262) in `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/services/tailscale_manager.rb`
- Docker error: `"network sandbox for container {ID} not found"`
- The error was already captured in your error tracking and `.entire/metadata` logs

**Flow diagram:**

```
SandboxProvisionJob#perform (line 19)
  ‚Üď
SandboxManager#create_container_and_start (lines 55-99)
  ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ Docker::Container.create() ‚ÄĒ creates container
  ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ container.start() ‚ÄĒ starts container (line 73)
  ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ container.refresh! ‚ÄĒ reloads container state (line 74)
  ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running") ‚ÄĒ SAVES container_id to DB (line 81)
  ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ NetworkManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox) ‚ÄĒ connects to per-user network (lines 84-91)

**Root cause is likely one of these factors:**

2. **Container ID is fresh but Docker's internal state isn't synced:** After `Docker::Container.create()` and `.start()`, the container ID is immediately saved to the DB and returned, but Docker's internal networking state may not have fully indexed the container yet.

  user.reload

end
```

rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e

file.content:
def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    ensure_user_network(user)
    user.reload

    return unless sandbox.container_id.present? && user.network_name.present?

    # Check if already connected before attempting (avoids swallowing real errors)
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    connected_networks = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return if connected_networks.key?(user.network_name)

    network = Docker::Network.get(user.network_name)
    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Error
    raise
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox #{sandbox.full_name} to user network: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Disconnect a sandbox container from its owner's per-user network.
  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present? && user.network_name.present?

Now here's the full picture:

**The error** `"network sandbox for container X not found"` is a Docker API error meaning the container's **network namespace** isn't ready yet. This is a Sysbox-specific timing issue ‚ÄĒ Sysbox containers have heavier initialization (user namespace, rootless daemon, etc.) so there's a lag between the Docker API reporting "Running" and the network namespace being attachable.

**Why `NetworkManager` doesn't fail:** It calls `Docker::Container.get()` first which forces a refresh, and by that point enough time has passed. It also checks if already connected.

**Why `TailscaleManager` fails:** It goes straight to `network.connect()` with no container verification or retry.

**A full fix would:**

1. **Add a container-ready check** ‚ÄĒ verify the container exists and is truly running before connecting
2. **Check if already connected** ‚ÄĒ skip redundant work (same pattern as NetworkManager)
3. **Retry with backoff** for the specific "network sandbox" error ‚ÄĒ Sysbox containers need a moment for their namespace to stabilize
4. **Keep the error path clean** ‚ÄĒ only raise after retries are exhausted

input.new_string:
def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    # Verify container exists and check if already connected
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    connected_networks = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return sandbox if connected_networks.key?(user.tailscale_network)

    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)

    # Sysbox containers have heavier init (user namespace, rootless daemon) so
    # the network namespace may not be attachable immediately after start.
    retries = 0
    begin
      network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
    rescue Docker::Error::ServerError => e
      raise unless e.message.include?("network sandbox") && retries < 3
      retries += 1
      sleep retries
      retry
    end

    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
    sandbox
  rescue Error
    raise
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

class TailscaleManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  TAILSCALE_IMAGE = "tailscale/tailscale:latest"
  TAILSCALE_TAG = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TAILSCALE_TAG", "").presence
  LOGIN_URL_PATTERN = %r{https://login\.tailscale\.com/\S+}

  # Stable Tailscale machine name for this server's sidecar containers.
  # Derived from SANDCASTLE_NAME (set in .env) or falls back to the system hostname.
  # Slugified: lowercase, non-alphanumeric runs ‚Üí "-", leading/trailing "-" stripped.
  TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME = begin
    name = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_NAME", "").presence || Socket.gethostname
    "sc-" + name.downcase.gsub(/[^a-z0-9]+/, "-").gsub(/\A-+|-+\z/, "")
  end

  class Error < StandardError; end

  # Restore sidecar from persisted tailscaled.state without re-authentication.
  # Used after reinstall or crash when interactive-login state survives on disk.
  # Note: we intentionally skip checking File.exist? for the state file because
  # the tailscale directory is root-owned (drwx------) and unreadable by the
  # sandcastle process. Containerboot will handle missing state gracefully.
  def restore_from_state(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?

    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    subnet = subnet_for(user)

    pull_image
    create_network(network_name, subnet)
    remove_existing_container(container_name)
    # Intentionally skip clear_tailscale_state ‚ÄĒ we rely on existing saved credentials
    # Use raw tailscaled (same as interactive flow) ‚ÄĒ NOT containerboot.
    # Containerboot runs `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, which would expire the
    # saved node key. Raw tailscaled exits cleanly without touching auth state.
    container = create_sidecar(
      name: container_name,
      user: user,
      network: network_name,
      subnet: subnet,
      auth_key: nil
    )
    container.start

    # Re-advertise routes after tailscaled reconnects ‚ÄĒ the subnet may have changed
    # after a reinstall (Docker reallocates from the pool). No --reset so we preserve
    # credentials and other settings in the persisted state. Also re-applies the
    # canonical hostname so it reflects the current SANDCASTLE_NAME.
    container.exec([
      "sh", "-c",
      "sleep 3 && tailscale up" \
      " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
      " --accept-routes" \
      " --hostname=#{TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME}" \
      " --timeout=60s &"
    ])

    user.update!(
      tailscale_state: "enabled",
      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
      tailscale_network: network_name,
      tailscale_subnet: subnet
    )
    user
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    raise Error, "Failed to restore Tailscale from state: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Legacy: one-shot enable with an auth key (still supported for automation)
  def enable(user:, auth_key:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?

    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: auth_key)

    user.update!(
      tailscale_state: "enabled",
      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
      tailscale_network: network_name
    )
    save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
    user
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    raise Error, "Failed to enable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Phase 1: create and start sidecar, return immediately.
  # Called from TailscaleLoginJob ‚ÄĒ user is already in "pending" state.
  def start_login(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled?

    # If there's an old container, clean up first
    cleanup_sidecar(user) if user.tailscale_container_id.present?

    hostname = Rails.cache.read("ts_hostname:#{user.id}")
    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: nil, hostname: hostname)

    user.update!(
      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
      tailscale_network: network_name
    )

    { status: "starting" }
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    raise Error, "Failed to start Tailscale login: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Phase 2: progressive login status check
  # Returns: { status: "starting" | "waiting_for_url" | "login_ready" | "authenticated" | "error" }
  def check_login(user:)
    raise Error, "No pending login" unless user.tailscale_pending?
    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    return { status: "starting", message: "Starting sidecar container..." } unless running

    # Kick off `tailscale up` in the background if not already done
    cache_key = "ts_login_started:#{user.id}"
    unless Rails.cache.read(cache_key)
      subnet = subnet_for(user)
      tag = Rails.cache.read("ts_tag:#{user.id}") || TAILSCALE_TAG
      tag_flag = tag ? " --advertise-tags=#{tag}" : ""
      container.exec([
        "sh", "-c",
        "tailscale up --reset" \
        " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
        "#{tag_flag}" \
        " --hostname=#{container.json.dig("Config", "Hostname")}" \
        " --timeout=120s &"
      ])
      Rails.cache.write(cache_key, true, expires_in: 5.minutes)
      return { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
    end

    # Check tailscale status for auth progress
    status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
    if status_out.first.any?
      ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
      case ts_status["BackendState"]
      when "Running"
        Rails.cache.delete(cache_key)
        user.update!(tailscale_state: "enabled", tailscale_auto_connect: true)
        ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
        tailscale_ip = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?
        return {
          status: "authenticated",
          tailscale_ip: tailscale_ip,
          hostname: ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName"),
          tailnet: ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
        }
      when "NeedsLogin"
        auth_url = ts_status["AuthURL"]
        if auth_url.present?
          return { status: "login_ready", login_url: auth_url, message: "Click the link to authenticate" }
        end
      end
    end

    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
  rescue JSON::ParserError
    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")
    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
    raise Error, "Sidecar container disappeared"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to check login: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disable(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" if user.tailscale_disabled?

    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")

    # Disconnect all sandboxes first
    user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true).find_each do |sandbox|
      disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    cleanup_sidecar(user)

    user.update!(
      tailscale_state: "disabled",
      tailscale_container_id: nil,
      tailscale_network: nil
    )
    delete_auth_key(user)
    user
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to disable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  def status(user:)
    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" unless user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")

    ts_sandboxes = user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true)
    sandbox_ips = ts_sandboxes.map do |sb|
      ip = sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sb)
      { name: sb.name, ip: ip }
    end

    result = {
      running: running,
      container_id: user.tailscale_container_id[0..11],
      network: user.tailscale_network,
      connected_sandboxes: ts_sandboxes.count,
      sandboxes: sandbox_ips
    }

    if running
      ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
      result[:tailscale_ip] = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?

      status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
      if status_out.first.any?
        begin
          ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
          result[:hostname] = ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName")
          result[:tailnet] = ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
          result[:online] = ts_status.dig("Self", "Online")
          tags = ts_status.dig("Self", "Tags")
          result[:tags] = tags if tags.present?
        rescue JSON::ParserError
          # Status not available yet
        end
      end
    else
      # Grab last 20 lines of container logs for debugging
      logs = container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, tail: 20)
      result[:logs] = logs.encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace).strip if logs.present?
      result[:exit_code] = container.json.dig("State", "ExitCode")
      result[:error_reason] = container.json.dig("State", "Error")
    end

    result
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found ‚ÄĒ try disabling and re-enabling Tailscale"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to get Tailscale status: #{e.message}"
  end

  def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    return sandbox unless sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_network.present?

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
        network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
        # Network/container already gone or container not connected ‚ÄĒ desired state reached
      end
    end

    sandbox.update!(tailscale: false)
    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to disconnect sandbox from Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  def sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    return nil unless sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.container_id.present? && user.tailscale_network.present?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", user.tailscale_network, "IPAddress")
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    nil
  end

  def auth_key_path(user)
    File.join(DATA_DIR, "users", user.name, "tailscale", ".auth_key")
  end

  private

  def save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
    path = auth_key_path(user)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(path))
    File.write(path, auth_key)
    File.chmod(0o600, path)
  end

  def delete_auth_key(user)
    path = auth_key_path(user)
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  rescue Errno::ENOENT
    # already gone
  end

  def create_and_start_sidecar(user:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    subnet = subnet_for(user)

    pull_image
    create_network(network_name, subnet)
    remove_existing_container(container_name)
    clear_tailscale_state(user)
    container = create_sidecar(
      name: container_name,
      user: user,
      network: network_name,
      subnet: subnet,
      auth_key: auth_key,
      hostname: hostname
    )
    container.start

    # Persist the subnet so it survives reinstalls (subnet_for reads this first).
    user.update_column(:tailscale_subnet, subnet)

    [ network_name, container ]
  end

  def remove_existing_container(name)
    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
    container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
    container.delete(force: true)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # No existing container
  end

  # Remove stale tailscaled.state so the daemon starts fresh.
  # Stale state causes tailscaled to immediately set up VPN routes before
  # authentication, which silently drops all normal internet traffic.
  def clear_tailscale_state(user)
    state_file = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale/tailscaled.state"
    File.delete(state_file) if File.exist?(state_file)
  rescue Errno::ENOENT
    # already gone
  end

  def cleanup_sidecar(user)
    # Try by stored container ID first, then by name as fallback
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    [user.tailscale_container_id, container_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
      begin
        container = Docker::Container.get(ref)
        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
        container.delete(force: true)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Already gone
      end
    end

    # Clean up network by stored name, then by convention
    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    [user.tailscale_network, network_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
      begin
        Docker::Network.get(ref).delete
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Already gone
      end
    end
  end

  def cleanup_on_failure(user)
    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    begin
      Docker::Container.get(container_name).delete(force: true)
    rescue StandardError
      nil
    end
    begin
      Docker::Network.get(network_name).delete
    rescue StandardError
      nil
    end
    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
  end

  def pull_image
    Docker::Image.get(TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{TAILSCALE_IMAGE} ‚ÄĒ check network connectivity"
  end

  def subnet_for(user)
    # 1. Use the subnet stored in the DB ‚ÄĒ stable across Docker/reinstalls
    return user.tailscale_subnet if user.tailscale_subnet.present?

    # 2. If the network already exists on Docker, read its actual subnet
    begin
      network = Docker::Network.get("sc-ts-net-#{user.name}")
      ipam = network.info.dig("IPAM", "Config")
      return ipam.first["Subnet"] if ipam&.first
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # Network doesn't exist yet ‚ÄĒ fall through to generate a random /24
    end

    # 3. Generate a random /24 from the pool (first allocation)
    base = ENV["DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"]
    if base
      parts = base.split("/").first.split(".").map(&:to_i)
    else
      parts = [ 10, rand(1..254), 0, 0 ]
    end
    parts[2] = rand(1..254)
    "#{parts[0]}.#{parts[1]}.#{parts[2]}.0/24"
  end

  def create_network(name, subnet)
    Docker::Network.get(name)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(
      name,
      "Driver" => "bridge",
      "IPAM" => {
        "Config" => [ { "Subnet" => subnet } ]
      }
    )
  end

  def create_sidecar(name:, user:, network:, subnet:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
    state_dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale"
    ts_hostname = hostname.presence || TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME

    config = {
      "Image" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE,
      "name" => name,
      "Hostname" => ts_hostname,
      "HostConfig" => {
        "NetworkMode" => network,
        "CapAdd" => [ "NET_ADMIN", "SYS_MODULE" ],
        "Devices" => [
          { "PathOnHost" => "/dev/net/tun", "PathInContainer" => "/dev/net/tun", "CgroupPermissions" => "rwm" }
        ],
        "Sysctls" => { "net.ipv4.ip_forward" => "1" },
        "Binds" => [ "#{state_dir}:/var/lib/tailscale" ],
        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
      }
    }

    if auth_key.present?
      # Use containerboot (default entrypoint) with auth key for automated flow
      config["Env"] = [
        "TS_STATE_DIR=/var/lib/tailscale",
        "TS_HOSTNAME=#{ts_hostname}",
        "TS_EXTRA_ARGS=--advertise-routes=#{subnet} --accept-routes#{TAILSCALE_TAG ? " --advertise-tags=#{TAILSCALE_TAG}" : ""}",
        "TS_AUTH_ONCE=true",
        "TS_AUTHKEY=#{auth_key}"
      ]
    else
      # Run tailscaled directly ‚ÄĒ we manage login via `tailscale up`.
      # Also used for restore_from_state: raw tailscaled exits without running
      # `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, preserving saved credentials in state file.
      config["Entrypoint"] = [ "tailscaled", "--state=/var/lib/tailscale/tailscaled.state" ]
      config["Cmd"] = []
    end

    Docker::Container.create(config)
  end
end

file.content:
def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    # Verify container exists and check if already connected
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    connected_networks = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return sandbox if connected_networks.key?(user.tailscale_network)

    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)

    # Sysbox containers have heavier init (user namespace, rootless daemon) so
    # the network namespace may not be attachable immediately after start.
    retries = 0
    begin
      network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
    rescue Docker::Error::ServerError => e
      raise unless e.message.include?("network sandbox") && retries < 3
      retries += 1
      sleep retries
      retry
    end

    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
    sandbox
  rescue Error
    raise
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"

content.text:
The fix:

1. **Container existence check** ‚ÄĒ `Docker::Container.get()` verifies the container is real
2. **Already-connected check** ‚ÄĒ skips if already on the Tailscale network (same pattern as `NetworkManager`)
3. **Retry with linear backoff** ‚ÄĒ catches the specific "network sandbox" error, retries up to 3 times (1s, 2s, 3s) to let Sysbox's namespace stabilize
4. **Clean error passthrough** ‚ÄĒ `Error` re-raised directly, other Docker errors wrapped

Want me to commit and release?

fix: retry Tailscale network connect for Sysbox namespace init delay

Sysbox containers have heavier initialization (user namespace, rootless
daemon) so the network namespace may not be attachable immediately after
start. Add container existence check, already-connected guard, and retry
with backoff matching the pattern in NetworkManager.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Two separate issues here:

1. **`thies-silent-hawk`** (no home mount) ‚ÄĒ fully working, connected to all networks including `sc-ts-net-thies` with IPs
2. **`thies-calm-phoenix`** (home mount) ‚ÄĒ restart loop with `chmod: changing permissions of '/home/thies': Value too large for defined data type`

The Tailscale network issue is a side effect ‚ÄĒ the container can't start, so it has no IPs on any network. The root cause is the `chmod` failure in the entrypoint. Let me check:

input.command: ssh sandman "sudo /sandcastle/dockyard/bin/docker inspect thies-calm-phoenix --format '{{json .HostConfig.Binds}}' 2>&1 | python3 -m json.tool"
